events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 Office letter
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955, with committee appointments and reports on conservation, reporting systems, soil and range surveys, and permit relations
Committee appointed at Land Operations Conference on June 14, 1955, chaired by K. S. Engel of Turtle Mountain
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 published amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of grazing fees from submarginal lands
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to Superintendents
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents attaching committee reports from Aberdeen Area Land Operations Conference
Transmittal from Billings Area Office Branch of Realty to Superintendent, North Cheyenne, attaching copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy
Central Office memorandum on fee patent policy referenced in November 7, 1955 transmittal
Central Office memorandum on disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoted in November 29, 1955 Billings Area memorandum
Billings Area Office memorandum to Superintendents and Officers in Charge regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents and Officers in Charge regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory survey conducted by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement, now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist, transferred from Standing Rock to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Formal soil survey work plan being completed with SCS for Shannon County on Pine Ridge Reservation

financial_transactions.description: Grazing fees collected for submarginal land grazing privileges held in special deposit pending legislation to transfer lands and fees to respective tribes

financial_transactions.payee: U.S. Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719
financial_transactions.description: Revenue from submarginal lands to be deposited to General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified, per amended 25 CFR 71.27

financial_transactions.payee: Bureau of Indian Affairs irrigation projects
financial_transactions.description: Construction, operation and maintenance charges required to be collected under Act of March 7, 1928 and 25 CFR 151; delinquent operation and maintenance charges must be deducted from land sale proceeds per bid form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee as equitable investment for lessees placing conservation improvements on Indian lands

relationships.subject: Reinholdt Hurst
relationships.object: Bureau of Indian Affairs Billings Area Office

relationships.subject: K. S. Engel
relationships.context: Land Operations Officer, served as committee chairman at Land Operations Conference

relationships.subject: Rex Carey

relationships.subject: Bill Smith
relationships.context: Soil Scientist on educational leave, to return to Rosebud

relationships.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: BLM called attention to deficiencies in patent requests; both agencies process patent issuances

relationships.subject: Billings Area Office
relationships.context: Area office issues directives and memoranda to superintendents

relationships.subject: Commissioner of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: Commissioner issued fee patent policy memorandum to area directors

relationships.subject: Soil Conservation Service
relationships.context: Conducted 1939 range inventory under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; cooperating on soil survey work plans

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for grazing allocations as non-Indian husbands were actual recipients of subsidy

relationships.subject: Act of March 7, 1928
relationships.context: Created first lien against irrigable lands for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs

relationships.subject: 25 CFR 71.27
relationships.context: Regulation amended August 10, 1955 to remove requirement to hold fees in special deposit

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in all patent requests so they can be handled expeditiously
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum distributed; Billings Area Office forwarded copy to superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Obtain necessary information relating to rights of way and include with transmittals for patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale are traversed by approved rights of way

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 10 and subsequent policy statements of June 3 and October 4, 1955
correspondence.action_requested: For information only - copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 attached

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Any funds held in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of calendar year; future revenue to be deposited to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Review attached committee reports and provide comments on conclusions reached by several committees
correspondence.outcome: Reports distributed with request for comment

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Each Superintendent to establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment collection requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs, directing lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands

Amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of submarginal land grazing fees in special deposit; funds to be deposited to Treasury miscellaneous receipt account
Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; special deposits were held in support of this legislation; no longer supported after August 10, 1955 amendment
